title: hypericum hypericoides subsp. multicaule
intro: Hypericum hypericoides subsp. multicaule is a subspecies of the St. Andrew's cross plant, native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its multiple stems and small, star-shaped flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and depression, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
